CIPHER.411.cipherString: People who pin their faith to a catchword never feel the necessity of understanding anything.
CIPHER.411.question: Agnes Repplier offers an observation that has been encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.411.author: Agnes Repplier

CIPHER.408.cipherString: Look to the day when we can say goodbye to all out heroes because their work is done. Justice is alive, independent, unassailable.
CIPHER.408.question: A laser-etched message on the inside of a geodes crystal reads: solve this Patristocrat version of Martin Firrell's wisdom using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with LOOK. What does it say?
CIPHER.408.author: Martin Firrell

CIPHER.400.cipherString: When we pay attention, we don't spend on it. But what we achieve is worth spending an entire life on.
CIPHER.400.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Kangoma Kindembo encoded as an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.400.author: Kangoma Kindembo

CIPHER.406.cipherString: He sought rest only in perpetual physical motion.
CIPHER.406.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by Alfred Deakin, written in a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of RETAIN and Polybius key of EMBARKING. What does it say?
CIPHER.406.author: Alfred Deakin

CIPHER.385.cipherString: The reader might reflect that an awful lot of supposing has to take place in order for the quantity theory of money to be true.
CIPHER.385.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote by Paul Ormerod encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with THE. What does it say?
CIPHER.385.author: Paul Ormerod

CIPHER.405.cipherString: Don't only think of succeeding in what you are doing but excel in it and use your position to solve problem.
CIPHER.405.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by Takyiwaa Manuh encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Key Phrase used to encode it?
CIPHER.405.author: Takyiwaa Manuh

CIPHER.396.cipherString: Human-machine symbiosis is the workforce of the future.
CIPHER.396.question: Found on a microchip inside a disassembled robotics kit: this Baconian Cipher by Newton Lee. What does it say?
CIPHER.396.author: Newton Lee

CIPHER.389.cipherString: For wolves and pigs and bears, thinking that they are human is a tragedy. For a cat, its an experience.
CIPHER.389.question: You find an encoded message in the test tube rack, written on masking tape. It's a quote by Witches Abroad, written in an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Key Phrase used to encode it?
CIPHER.389.author: Witches Abroad

CIPHER.402.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote using the Cryptarithm Cipher. What do the values 748 9568 01324 decode to?

CIPHER.409.cipherString: The distance is nothing; it is only the first step that is difficult.
CIPHER.409.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Madame du Deffand, encoded as a Porta using a keyword of LOAFING. What does it say?
CIPHER.409.author: Madame du Deffand

CIPHER.388.cipherString: In the midst of life we are in debt.
CIPHER.388.question: Ethel Watts Mumford has been heard to say the following phrase that has been encoded using the Hill Cipher with a keyword of PROTOTYPE. What does it say?
CIPHER.388.author: Ethel Watts Mumford

CIPHER.399.cipherString: No matter how good an idea sounds, test it first.
CIPHER.399.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Henry Bloch, encrypted as a Baconian. You are told that the deciphered text starts with NOMA. What does it say?
CIPHER.399.author: Henry Bloch

CIPHER.403.cipherString: Multiple exclamation marks, he went on, shaking his head, are a sure sign of a diseased mind.
CIPHER.403.question: Someone left a paper with the message below on the copy machine in the office. It's a quote by Terry Pratchett, encoded as a Checkerboard. The 30th through 39th cipher units (SG SU TG NH SH TT OH TU TG TH) decode to be TONSHAKING. What does it say?
CIPHER.403.author: Terry Pratchett

CIPHER.391.cipherString: People never realize how much work impacts their self esteem and sense of purpose until they leave a job.
CIPHER.391.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Rob Payne, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.391.author: Rob Payne

CIPHER.392.cipherString: Never agree to plastic surgery if the doctor's office is full of portraits by Picasso.
CIPHER.392.question: Your microscope slide prep strangely revealed this Checkerboard message with a Polybius key of HYSTERICAL. What does it say?
CIPHER.392.author: Unknown

CIPHER.397.cipherString: I asked my friend for a sharpened pencil, but he didn't have one. I always knew he was a little dull...
CIPHER.397.question: A message was left on the whiteboard after science club. It's a quote encoded using an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher starts with I. What does it say?
CIPHER.397.author: Unknown

CIPHER.398.cipherString: Pay attention to the intricate patterns of your existence that you take for granted.
CIPHER.398.question: Your graphing calculator won’t stop displaying this Checkerboard puzzle by Doug Dillon with a Polybius key of SPLENDOR. What does it say?
CIPHER.398.author: Doug Dillon

CIPHER.407.cipherString: If you can dream it, you can do it. Never give up on your dreams.
CIPHER.407.question: Your lava lamp started bubbling in Baconian code by John Basedow. What does it say?
CIPHER.407.author: John Basedow

CIPHER.383.cipherString: La honestidad es la mejor política; pero el que se rige por esa máxima no es un hombre honrado.
CIPHER.383.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You are told it's a quote by Richard Whately encoded as a Xenocrypt in Spanish using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.383.author: Richard Whately
CIPHER.383.translation: Honesty is the best policy; but he who is governed by that maxim is not an honest man.

CIPHER.394.cipherString: Education makes your maths better, not necessarily your manners.
CIPHER.394.question: Hidden in the margin of a library book on black holes: decode this Complete Columnar version of Amit Kalantri. The quote has BETTER somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.394.author: Amit Kalantri

CIPHER.395.cipherString: One does not discover new lands without consenting to lose sight of the shore for a very long time.
CIPHER.395.question: Frozen inside an ice core sample from Antarctica: this Aristocrat message by Andre Gide. You are told that the cipher contains the word NOT. What does it say?
CIPHER.395.author: Andre Gide

CIPHER.384.cipherString: But man is not made for defeat. A man can be destroyed but not defeated.
CIPHER.384.question: The janitor found this strange paper strip under a Bunsen burner. It's a quote, encoded with a Nihilist Substitution Cipher with a keyword length of 6. The 12th through 23rd cipher units (66 29 45 47 55 84 39 27 64 48 54 56) decode to be MADEFORDEFEA. What does it say?

CIPHER.410.cipherString: We know from science that nothing in the universe exists as an isolated or independent entity.
CIPHER.410.question: Scratched onto the lens of an abandoned telescope, decode this Aristocrat quote by Margaret J. Wheatley. What does it say?
CIPHER.410.author: Margaret J. Wheatley

CIPHER.401.cipherString: Adaptability is a most desirable habit or quality for success in life.
CIPHER.401.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Sivananda Saraswati, encoded as a Porta. The 36th through 42nd cipher characters (DLXRWLL) decode to be QUALITY. What does it say?
CIPHER.401.author: Sivananda Saraswati

CIPHER.387.cipherString: If our opinions rest upon solid ground, those who attack them do not make us angry, but themselves ridiculous.
CIPHER.387.question: Etched into the surface of a melted vinyl record, you found this Aristocrat-encrypted quote by John Lancaster Spalding. What does it say?
CIPHER.387.author: John Lancaster Spalding

CIPHER.386.cipherString: When someone says to me, no one's done that before, I say: great, then I can be the first.
CIPHER.386.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote by Jonas Deichmann, written using a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has SDONE somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.386.author: Jonas Deichmann

CIPHER.393.cipherString: Infeliz es el hombre por quien su propia madre no ha hecho venerables a todas las demás madres.
CIPHER.393.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by RICHTER in Spanish, written as a Xenocrypt Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.393.author: RICHTER
CIPHER.393.translation: Unhappy is the man for whom his own mother has not made all other mothers venerable.

CIPHER.390.question: A message encrypted with the Cryptarithm Cipher has been received. What do the values 10234 7851 983 456 decode to?

CIPHER.404.cipherString: Men lived like fishes; the great ones devoured the small.
CIPHER.404.question: You receive this puzzling text during a live science broadcast. It contains a quote by Algernon Sidney, encrypted as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of TRASHY and Polybius key of DANGEROUSLY. What does it say?
CIPHER.404.author: Algernon Sidney

CIPHER.382.question: Found inside a dissected frog's stomach (plastic, don’t worry): this Hill encoded note by David Lean using a keyword of NEXT. What does it say?
CIPHER.382.author: David Lean
